title: US gas prices are back up to $4 a gallon | AP News
excerpt: U.S. gas prices have jumped to an average of $4 a gallon again as the U.S. and Iran launch more attacks. According to AAA, the national average for a gallon of regular gasoline is now back to $4. The average price a year ago was $3.14 a gallon. The price is a national average, meaning drivers in some states have been paying well over $4 a gallon for a while now. Prices vary between states due to factors ranging from nearby supply to differing tax rates. People around the world are also dealing with high gas prices as a result of the war.
content_html:
NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. gas prices jumped back to $4 a gallon on average Monday, as Washington’s latest fighting with Iran renews strain on the flow of fuel worldwide.

According to motor club AAA, the national average for a gallon of regular gasoline is now just above the $4 mark. That’s about 13 cents more expensive than it was a week ago, and far higher than where prices at the pump stood at this time last year, when American drivers could fill up for about $3.14 a gallon on average.

The U.S. and Israel’s war with Iran plunged the world into a global energy crisis, notably as the cost of crude oil — the main ingredient in gasoline — soared due to supply chain and production disruptions across the Middle East.

Energy prices cooled last month when Washington and Tehran reached an interim agreement aimed at permanently ending their fighting and reviving traffic in the key Strait of Hormuz. But that peace deal has now crumbled — with the U.S. on Monday only intensifying airstrikes against Iran, which has responded by launching its own attacks on American allies in the region. And oil flows are largely stalling again.

Many eyes are on the White House. Before co-launching the war in late February, President Donald Trump once bragged about keeping gas prices low — and expressed frustration about them not falling as quickly as the cost of crude oil last month. If prices continue to rebound, expensive gas will likely be at the top of voters’ minds during the midterm elections this November.

Here’s what we know.

Where gas prices stand today

Again, U.S. gas prices are now back at the $4 mark. But that figure is a national average, meaning drivers in some states have been paying well over $4 a gallon for a while now, while others pay less.

California on Monday continued to see the most expensive gas, with an average of nearly $5.50 a gallon — followed by almost $5.42 in Hawaii and $5.01 in Washington state, per AAA. Meanwhile, states like Indiana and Mississippi have averages closer to $3.35 and $3.57, respectively. Prices vary between due to factors ranging from nearby supply to differing tax rates.

Crude oil is the main ingredient in gasoline, and its cost makes up the bulk of what consumers pay at the pump. There’s a bit of a delay because refineries buy crude in advance, and deliveries of that oil take time. But the latest gas prices follow an uptick in oil costs seen over recent weeks. The price of Brent crude, the international standard, swung between roughly $86 to $91 a barrel Monday — compared to closer to $72 at the start of July.

Oil has been volatile throughout the course of the war, with prices soaring more rapidly in the conflict’s early days. In March, Brent climbed as high as nearly $120 per barrel. Meanwhile, U.S. gas prices hit a four-year record of more than $4.50 a gallon in May.

The national average fell to as low as $3.79 a gallon earlier this month. And the latest turnaround is bringing whiplash to some drivers.

“I am down to the last drops of gas,” said Litza Mavrothalasitis, a Chicago resident, when filling her tank on Monday, adding that she was shocked to see how high it had gone up again. “It was just unbelievable. I thought we were finally settling down.”

Sign up for Morning Wire: Our flagship newsletter breaks down the biggest headlines of the day.

Jacob Fisher, another Chicago resident who was buying gas on Monday, added that thinking about what’s behind these higher fuel costs “makes me angry.”

“Senseless war in Iran, and our current administration is fueling a lot of this — and it’s terrible,” Fisher said. “It is not serving the American people. Nor does it serve anywhere in the entire world.”

White House’s response and the road ahead

White House spokesperson Taylor Rogers said in a statement Monday that “oil and gas prices will plummet back to pre-conflict levels” as the U.S. military degrades Iran’s ability to attack vessels and disrupt trade in the Strait of Hormuz, where roughly a fifth of the world’s oil sailed through during peacetime.

“President Trump remains committed to unleashing American energy dominance, cutting costs, and putting more money back in the pockets of hardworking American families,” Rogers added.

Still, the escalation of attacks and confusion over future control of Hormuz has fueled uncertainty. All the while, traffic in the key waterway has stalled — with analysts from S&P Global Energy noting Monday that vessel crossings fell 50% last week compared to the week prior.

“The continued predominance of Iran-linked and sanctioned vessels suggests mainstream shipowners remain cautious about transiting the waterway,” the analysts added.

Even if fighting ends, experts have repeatedly warned it could take months, if not longer, for supply chains to return to prewar levels. Last month, S&P Global Energy said it did not expect Persian Gulf oil production to rebound fully until at least the first quarter of 2027.

Gas isn’t the only necessity getting more expensive

Beyond gasoline, fuel like diesel — which powers many freight and long-haul trucks — is also back on the rise. On Monday, diesel averaged at nearly $5.11 a gallon in the U.S., per AAA, up from $4.88 just a week ago.

American households have already collectively paid nearly $71.1 billion in extra costs for gas and diesel alone since the war began, according to an online tracker from Brown University’s Watson School of International and Public Affairs.

Beyond pain at the pump, higher fuel prices raise transportation costs across businesses. And shortages of other core necessities — like fertilizer — have also worsened amid the war. Groceries, airline tickets and even goods like condoms and shoes have gotten more expensive in recent months.

Supply shocks have been even starker in places that rely more heavily on imports from the Middle East — notably countries across Asia and Africa.

______

AP journalists Laura Bargfeld in Chicago and Matthew Daly in Washington contributed to this report.

blocks.html_en: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. gas prices jumped back to $4 a gallon on average Monday, as Washington’s latest fighting with Iran renews strain on the flow of fuel worldwide.

sentences.en: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. gas prices jumped back to $4 a gallon on average Monday, as Washington’s latest fighting with Iran renews strain on the flow of fuel worldwide.
sentences.zh: 纽约（美联社）——美国天然气价格周一平均回升至每加仑 4 美元，因为华盛顿与伊朗的最新战斗再次给全球燃料供应带来压力。

blocks.html_en: According to motor club AAA, the national average for a gallon of regular gasoline is now just above the $4 mark. That’s about 13 cents more expensive than it was a week ago, and far higher than where prices at the pump stood at this time last year, when American drivers could fill up for about $3.14 a gallon on average.

sentences.en: According to motor club AAA, the national average for a gallon of regular gasoline is now just above the $4 mark.
sentences.zh: 根据 AAA 汽车俱乐部的数据，全国每加仑普通汽油的平均价格目前略高于 4 美元。

sentences.en: That’s about 13 cents more expensive than it was a week ago, and far higher than where prices at the pump stood at this time last year, when American drivers could fill up for about $3.14 a gallon on average.
sentences.zh: 这比一周前贵了约 13 美分，远高于去年这个时候的加油价格，当时美国司机平均每加仑加油 3.14 美元左右。

blocks.html_en: The U.S. and Israel’s war with Iran plunged the world into a global energy crisis, notably as the cost of crude oil — the main ingredient in gasoline — soared due to supply chain and production disruptions across the Middle East.

sentences.en: The U.S. and Israel’s war with Iran plunged the world into a global energy crisis, notably as the cost of crude oil — the main ingredient in gasoline — soared due to supply chain and production disruptions across the Middle East.
sentences.zh: 美国和以色列与伊朗的战争使世界陷入全球能源危机，特别是由于中东地区的供应链和生产中断，原油（汽油的主要成分）成本飙升。

blocks.html_en: Energy prices cooled last month when Washington and Tehran reached an interim agreement aimed at permanently ending their fighting and reviving traffic in the key Strait of Hormuz. But that peace deal has now crumbled — with the U.S. on Monday only intensifying airstrikes against Iran, which has responded by launching its own attacks on American allies in the region. And oil flows are largely stalling again.

sentences.en: Energy prices cooled last month when Washington and Tehran reached an interim agreement aimed at permanently ending their fighting and reviving traffic in the key Strait of Hormuz.
sentences.zh: 上个月，华盛顿和德黑兰达成临时协议，旨在永久结束双方的战斗并恢复重要的霍尔木兹海峡的交通，能源价格降温。

sentences.en: But that peace deal has now crumbled — with the U.S. on Monday only intensifying airstrikes against Iran, which has responded by launching its own attacks on American allies in the region.
sentences.zh: 但这项和平协议现在已经破裂——美国周一只是加强了对伊朗的空袭，而伊朗则对美国在该地区的盟友发动了袭击作为回应。

sentences.en: And oil flows are largely stalling again.
sentences.zh: 石油流动再次基本陷入停滞。

blocks.html_en: Many eyes are on the White House. Before co-launching the war in late February, President Donald Trump once bragged about keeping gas prices low — and expressed frustration about them not falling as quickly as the cost of crude oil last month. If prices continue to rebound, expensive gas will likely be at the top of voters’ minds during the midterm elections this November.

sentences.en: Many eyes are on the White House.
sentences.zh: 许多目光都集中在白宫身上。

sentences.en: Before co-launching the war in late February, President Donald Trump once bragged about keeping gas prices low — and expressed frustration about them not falling as quickly as the cost of crude oil last month.
sentences.zh: 在二月底共同发动战争之前，唐纳德·特朗普总统曾吹嘘要保持较低的天然气价格，并对天然气价格没有像上个月原油价格那样迅速下跌表示失望。

sentences.en: If prices continue to rebound, expensive gas will likely be at the top of voters’ minds during the midterm elections this November.
sentences.zh: 如果价格继续反弹，昂贵的天然气可能会成为今年 11 月中期选举期间选民最关心的问题。

blocks.html_en: Here’s what we know.

sentences.en: Here’s what we know.
sentences.zh: 这是我们所知道的。

blocks.html_en: Where gas prices stand today

sentences.en: Where gas prices stand today
sentences.zh: 今天的天然气价格

blocks.html_en: Again, U.S. gas prices are now back at the $4 mark. But that figure is a national average, meaning drivers in some states have been paying well over $4 a gallon for a while now, while others pay less.

sentences.en: Again, U.S. gas prices are now back at the $4 mark.
sentences.zh: 美国天然气价格现在再次回到 4 美元大关。

sentences.en: But that figure is a national average, meaning drivers in some states have been paying well over $4 a gallon for a while now, while others pay less.
sentences.zh: 但这个数字是全国平均水平，这意味着一些州的司机一段时间以来支付的费用远高于每加仑 4 美元，而其他州的支付费用则较低。

blocks.html_en: California on Monday continued to see the most expensive gas, with an average of nearly $5.50 a gallon — followed by almost $5.42 in Hawaii and $5.01 in Washington state, per AAA. Meanwhile, states like Indiana and Mississippi have averages closer to $3.35 and $3.57, respectively. Prices vary between due to factors ranging from nearby supply to differing tax rates.

sentences.en: California on Monday continued to see the most expensive gas, with an average of nearly $5.50 a gallon — followed by almost $5.42 in Hawaii and $5.01 in Washington state, per AAA.
sentences.zh: 周一，加利福尼亚州的天然气价格继续最高，根据 AAA，平均价格接近每加仑 5.50 美元，其次是夏威夷州的近 5.42 美元和华盛顿州的 5.01 美元。

sentences.en: Meanwhile, states like Indiana and Mississippi have averages closer to $3.35 and $3.57, respectively.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，印第安纳州和密西西比州等州的平均房价分别接近 3.35 美元和 3.57 美元。

sentences.en: Prices vary between due to factors ranging from nearby supply to differing tax rates.
sentences.zh: 由于附近供应和不同税率等因素，价格有所不同。

blocks.html_en: Crude oil is the main ingredient in gasoline, and its cost makes up the bulk of what consumers pay at the pump. There’s a bit of a delay because refineries buy crude in advance, and deliveries of that oil take time. But the latest gas prices follow an uptick in oil costs seen over recent weeks. The price of Brent crude, the international standard, swung between roughly $86 to $91 a barrel Monday — compared to closer to $72 at the start of July.

sentences.en: Crude oil is the main ingredient in gasoline, and its cost makes up the bulk of what consumers pay at the pump.
sentences.zh: 原油是汽油的主要成分，其成本占消费者在加油站支付的大部分费用。

sentences.en: There’s a bit of a delay because refineries buy crude in advance, and deliveries of that oil take time.
sentences.zh: 由于炼油厂提前购买原油，而石油的交付需要时间，因此会出现一些延迟。

sentences.en: But the latest gas prices follow an uptick in oil costs seen over recent weeks.
sentences.zh: 但最新的天然气价格是在最近几周石油成本上涨之后出现的。

sentences.en: The price of Brent crude, the international standard, swung between roughly $86 to $91 a barrel Monday — compared to closer to $72 at the start of July.
sentences.zh: 周一，国际标准布伦特原油价格在每桶约 86 美元至 91 美元之间波动，而 7 月初的价格接近每桶 72 美元。

blocks.html_en: Oil has been volatile throughout the course of the war, with prices soaring more rapidly in the conflict’s early days. In March, Brent climbed as high as nearly $120 per barrel. Meanwhile, U.S. gas prices hit a four-year record of more than $4.50 a gallon in May.

sentences.en: Oil has been volatile throughout the course of the war, with prices soaring more rapidly in the conflict’s early days.
sentences.zh: 在整个战争过程中，石油价格一直波动较大，冲突初期价格飙升得更快。

sentences.en: In March, Brent climbed as high as nearly $120 per barrel.
sentences.zh: 3 月份，布伦特原油价格攀升至每桶近 120 美元。

sentences.en: Meanwhile, U.S. gas prices hit a four-year record of more than $4.50 a gallon in May.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，5 月份美国天然气价格创下四年来最高纪录，超过每加仑 4.50 美元。

blocks.html_en: The national average fell to as low as $3.79 a gallon earlier this month. And the latest turnaround is bringing whiplash to some drivers.

sentences.en: The national average fell to as low as $3.79 a gallon earlier this month.
sentences.zh: 本月早些时候，全国平均价格跌至每加仑 3.79 美元。

sentences.en: And the latest turnaround is bringing whiplash to some drivers.
sentences.zh: 最新的转变给一些司机带来了痛苦。

blocks.html_en: “I am down to the last drops of gas,” said Litza Mavrothalasitis, a Chicago resident, when filling her tank on Monday, adding that she was shocked to see how high it had gone up again. “It was just unbelievable. I thought we were finally settling down.”

sentences.en: “I am down to the last drops of gas,” said Litza Mavrothalasitis, a Chicago resident, when filling her tank on Monday, adding that she was shocked to see how high it had gone up again.
sentences.zh: “我已经耗尽了最后一滴汽油，”周一，芝加哥居民利扎·马夫罗萨拉斯蒂斯 (Litza Mavrothalasitis) 在给她的油箱加油时说道，她补充说，她很惊讶地发现油箱里的油又升了那么高。

sentences.en: “It was just unbelievable.
sentences.zh: “这真是令人难以置信。

sentences.en: I thought we were finally settling down.”
sentences.zh: 我以为我们终于安定下来了。”

sentences.en: Sign up for Morning Wire: Our flagship newsletter breaks down the biggest headlines of the day.
sentences.zh: 订阅 Morning Wire：我们的旗舰时事通讯详细介绍了当天的头条新闻。

blocks.html_en: Jacob Fisher, another Chicago resident who was buying gas on Monday, added that thinking about what’s behind these higher fuel costs “makes me angry.”

sentences.en: Jacob Fisher, another Chicago resident who was buying gas on Monday, added that thinking about what’s behind these higher fuel costs “makes me angry.”
sentences.zh: 周一购买天然气的另一位芝加哥居民雅各布·费希尔补充说，思考燃料成本上涨背后的原因“让我很生气”。

blocks.html_en: “Senseless war in Iran, and our current administration is fueling a lot of this — and it’s terrible,” Fisher said. “It is not serving the American people. Nor does it serve anywhere in the entire world.”

sentences.en: “Senseless war in Iran, and our current administration is fueling a lot of this — and it’s terrible,” Fisher said.
sentences.zh: 费舍尔说：“伊朗发生了毫无意义的战争，而我们现任政府正在助长这种战争，这太可怕了。”

sentences.en: “It is not serving the American people.
sentences.zh: “这不是为美国人民服务。

sentences.en: Nor does it serve anywhere in the entire world.”
sentences.zh: 它也不服务于全世界任何地方。”

blocks.html_en: White House’s response and the road ahead

sentences.en: White House’s response and the road ahead
sentences.zh: 白宫的回应和未来的道路

blocks.html_en: White House spokesperson Taylor Rogers said in a statement Monday that “oil and gas prices will plummet back to pre-conflict levels” as the U.S. military degrades Iran’s ability to attack vessels and disrupt trade in the Strait of Hormuz, where roughly a fifth of the world’s oil sailed through during peacetime.

sentences.en: White House spokesperson Taylor Rogers said in a statement Monday that “oil and gas prices will plummet back to pre-conflict levels” as the U.S. military degrades Iran’s ability to attack vessels and disrupt trade in the Strait of Hormuz, where roughly a fifth of the world’s oil sailed through during peacetime.
sentences.zh: 白宫发言人泰勒·罗杰斯周一在一份声明中表示，随着美国军队削弱伊朗攻击船只和扰乱霍尔木兹海峡贸易的能力，“石油和天然气价格将暴跌至冲突前水平”。和平时期，世界上大约五分之一的石油通过该海峡运输。

blocks.html_en: “President Trump remains committed to unleashing American energy dominance, cutting costs, and putting more money back in the pockets of hardworking American families,” Rogers added.

sentences.en: “President Trump remains committed to unleashing American energy dominance, cutting costs, and putting more money back in the pockets of hardworking American families,” Rogers added.
sentences.zh: 罗杰斯补充道：“特朗普总统仍然致力于释放美国的能源主导地位，削减成本，并将更多的钱放回勤劳的美国家庭的口袋里。”

blocks.html_en: Still, the escalation of attacks and confusion over future control of Hormuz has fueled uncertainty. All the while, traffic in the key waterway has stalled — with analysts from S&P Global Energy noting Monday that vessel crossings fell 50% last week compared to the week prior.

sentences.en: Still, the escalation of attacks and confusion over future control of Hormuz has fueled uncertainty.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，袭击的升级以及对霍尔木兹未来控制的混乱加剧了不确定性。

sentences.en: All the while, traffic in the key waterway has stalled — with analysts from S&P Global Energy noting Monday that vessel crossings fell 50% last week compared to the week prior.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，主要水道的交通一直停滞不前——标准普尔全球能源分析师周一指出，上周船舶过境量比前一周下降了 50%。

blocks.html_en: “The continued predominance of Iran-linked and sanctioned vessels suggests mainstream shipowners remain cautious about transiting the waterway,” the analysts added.

sentences.en: “The continued predominance of Iran-linked and sanctioned vessels suggests mainstream shipowners remain cautious about transiting the waterway,” the analysts added.
sentences.zh: 分析师补充说：“与伊朗有关和受制裁的船只持续占据主导地位，这表明主流船东对通过该水道仍持谨慎态度。”

blocks.html_en: Even if fighting ends, experts have repeatedly warned it could take months, if not longer, for supply chains to return to prewar levels. Last month, S&P Global Energy said it did not expect Persian Gulf oil production to rebound fully until at least the first quarter of 2027.

sentences.en: Even if fighting ends, experts have repeatedly warned it could take months, if not longer, for supply chains to return to prewar levels.
sentences.zh: 即使战斗结束，专家们也一再警告称，供应链可能需要几个月甚至更长的时间才能恢复到战前水平。

sentences.en: Last month, S&P Global Energy said it did not expect Persian Gulf oil production to rebound fully until at least the first quarter of 2027.
sentences.zh: 上个月，标准普尔全球能源公司表示，预计波斯湾石油产量至少要到 2027 年第一季度才会全面反弹。

blocks.html_en: Gas isn’t the only necessity getting more expensive

sentences.en: Gas isn’t the only necessity getting more expensive
sentences.zh: 天然气并不是唯一变得更加昂贵的必需品

blocks.html_en: Beyond gasoline, fuel like diesel — which powers many freight and long-haul trucks — is also back on the rise. On Monday, diesel averaged at nearly $5.11 a gallon in the U.S., per AAA, up from $4.88 just a week ago.

sentences.en: Beyond gasoline, fuel like diesel — which powers many freight and long-haul trucks — is also back on the rise.
sentences.zh: 除了汽油之外，为许多货运和长途卡车提供动力的柴油等燃料的价格也重新回升。

sentences.en: On Monday, diesel averaged at nearly $5.11 a gallon in the U.S., per AAA, up from $4.88 just a week ago.
sentences.zh: 周一，美国柴油均价接近 AAA 每加仑 5.11 美元，而一周前为 4.88 美元。

blocks.html_en: American households have already collectively paid nearly $71.1 billion in extra costs for gas and diesel alone since the war began, according to an online tracker from Brown University’s Watson School of International and Public Affairs.

sentences.en: American households have already collectively paid nearly $71.1 billion in extra costs for gas and diesel alone since the war began, according to an online tracker from Brown University’s Watson School of International and Public Affairs.
sentences.zh: 布朗大学沃森国际与公共事务学院的在线追踪数据显示，自战争爆发以来，美国家庭仅在汽油和柴油方面就已经总共支付了近 711 亿美元的额外费用。

blocks.html_en: Beyond pain at the pump, higher fuel prices raise transportation costs across businesses. And shortages of other core necessities — like fertilizer — have also worsened amid the war. Groceries, airline tickets and even goods like condoms and shoes have gotten more expensive in recent months.

sentences.en: Beyond pain at the pump, higher fuel prices raise transportation costs across businesses.
sentences.zh: 除了加油带来的痛苦之外，燃油价格上涨还提高了企业的运输成本。

sentences.en: And shortages of other core necessities — like fertilizer — have also worsened amid the war.
sentences.zh: 战争期间，化肥等其他核心必需品的短缺也加剧了。

sentences.en: Groceries, airline tickets and even goods like condoms and shoes have gotten more expensive in recent months.
sentences.zh: 近几个月来，杂货、机票甚至避孕套和鞋子等商品都变得更加昂贵。

blocks.html_en: Supply shocks have been even starker in places that rely more heavily on imports from the Middle East — notably countries across Asia and Africa.

sentences.en: Supply shocks have been even starker in places that rely more heavily on imports from the Middle East — notably countries across Asia and Africa.
sentences.zh: 在更加依赖中东进口的地区，尤其是亚洲和非洲国家，供应冲击更为严重。

blocks.html_en: AP journalists Laura Bargfeld in Chicago and Matthew Daly in Washington contributed to this report.

sentences.en: AP journalists Laura Bargfeld in Chicago and Matthew Daly in Washington contributed to this report.
sentences.zh: 美联社驻芝加哥记者劳拉·巴格菲尔德和驻华盛顿记者马修·达利对此报道做出了贡献。

content_html_zh:
纽约（美联社）——美国天然气价格周一平均回升至每加仑 4 美元，因为华盛顿与伊朗的最新战斗再次给全球燃料供应带来压力。

根据 AAA 汽车俱乐部的数据，全国每加仑普通汽油的平均价格目前略高于 4 美元。 这比一周前贵了约 13 美分，远高于去年这个时候的加油价格，当时美国司机平均每加仑加油 3.14 美元左右。

美国和以色列与伊朗的战争使世界陷入全球能源危机，特别是由于中东地区的供应链和生产中断，原油（汽油的主要成分）成本飙升。

上个月，华盛顿和德黑兰达成临时协议，旨在永久结束双方的战斗并恢复重要的霍尔木兹海峡的交通，能源价格降温。 但这项和平协议现在已经破裂——美国周一只是加强了对伊朗的空袭，而伊朗则对美国在该地区的盟友发动了袭击作为回应。 石油流动再次基本陷入停滞。

许多目光都集中在白宫身上。 在二月底共同发动战争之前，唐纳德·特朗普总统曾吹嘘要保持较低的天然气价格，并对天然气价格没有像上个月原油价格那样迅速下跌表示失望。 如果价格继续反弹，昂贵的天然气可能会成为今年 11 月中期选举期间选民最关心的问题。

这是我们所知道的。

今天的天然气价格

美国天然气价格现在再次回到 4 美元大关。 但这个数字是全国平均水平，这意味着一些州的司机一段时间以来支付的费用远高于每加仑 4 美元，而其他州的支付费用则较低。

周一，加利福尼亚州的天然气价格继续最高，根据 AAA，平均价格接近每加仑 5.50 美元，其次是夏威夷州的近 5.42 美元和华盛顿州的 5.01 美元。 与此同时，印第安纳州和密西西比州等州的平均房价分别接近 3.35 美元和 3.57 美元。 由于附近供应和不同税率等因素，价格有所不同。

原油是汽油的主要成分，其成本占消费者在加油站支付的大部分费用。 由于炼油厂提前购买原油，而石油的交付需要时间，因此会出现一些延迟。 但最新的天然气价格是在最近几周石油成本上涨之后出现的。 周一，国际标准布伦特原油价格在每桶约 86 美元至 91 美元之间波动，而 7 月初的价格接近每桶 72 美元。

在整个战争过程中，石油价格一直波动较大，冲突初期价格飙升得更快。 3 月份，布伦特原油价格攀升至每桶近 120 美元。 与此同时，5 月份美国天然气价格创下四年来最高纪录，超过每加仑 4.50 美元。

本月早些时候，全国平均价格跌至每加仑 3.79 美元。 最新的转变给一些司机带来了痛苦。

“我已经耗尽了最后一滴汽油，”周一，芝加哥居民利扎·马夫罗萨拉斯蒂斯 (Litza Mavrothalasitis) 在给她的油箱加油时说道，她补充说，她很惊讶地发现油箱里的油又升了那么高。 “这真是令人难以置信。 我以为我们终于安定下来了。”

订阅 Morning Wire：我们的旗舰时事通讯详细介绍了当天的头条新闻。

周一购买天然气的另一位芝加哥居民雅各布·费希尔补充说，思考燃料成本上涨背后的原因“让我很生气”。

费舍尔说：“伊朗发生了毫无意义的战争，而我们现任政府正在助长这种战争，这太可怕了。” “这不是为美国人民服务。 它也不服务于全世界任何地方。”

白宫的回应和未来的道路

白宫发言人泰勒·罗杰斯周一在一份声明中表示，随着美国军队削弱伊朗攻击船只和扰乱霍尔木兹海峡贸易的能力，“石油和天然气价格将暴跌至冲突前水平”。和平时期，世界上大约五分之一的石油通过该海峡运输。

罗杰斯补充道：“特朗普总统仍然致力于释放美国的能源主导地位，削减成本，并将更多的钱放回勤劳的美国家庭的口袋里。”

尽管如此，袭击的升级以及对霍尔木兹未来控制的混乱加剧了不确定性。 与此同时，主要水道的交通一直停滞不前——标准普尔全球能源分析师周一指出，上周船舶过境量比前一周下降了 50%。

分析师补充说：“与伊朗有关和受制裁的船只持续占据主导地位，这表明主流船东对通过该水道仍持谨慎态度。”

即使战斗结束，专家们也一再警告称，供应链可能需要几个月甚至更长的时间才能恢复到战前水平。 上个月，标准普尔全球能源公司表示，预计波斯湾石油产量至少要到 2027 年第一季度才会全面反弹。

天然气并不是唯一变得更加昂贵的必需品

除了汽油之外，为许多货运和长途卡车提供动力的柴油等燃料的价格也重新回升。 周一，美国柴油均价接近 AAA 每加仑 5.11 美元，而一周前为 4.88 美元。

布朗大学沃森国际与公共事务学院的在线追踪数据显示，自战争爆发以来，美国家庭仅在汽油和柴油方面就已经总共支付了近 711 亿美元的额外费用。

除了加油带来的痛苦之外，燃油价格上涨还提高了企业的运输成本。 战争期间，化肥等其他核心必需品的短缺也加剧了。 近几个月来，杂货、机票甚至避孕套和鞋子等商品都变得更加昂贵。

在更加依赖中东进口的地区，尤其是亚洲和非洲国家，供应冲击更为严重。

______

美联社驻芝加哥记者劳拉·巴格菲尔德和驻华盛顿记者马修·达利对此报道做出了贡献。
